title: Heresay Homicide — Chapter 4: The Object That Should Not Exist
venue: Heresay Speakeasy at Circa 1857 Antique Store, Baton Rouge
description: Chapter 4: The Object That Should Not Exist in one continuous Heresay Homicide mystery. Focus: layered physical evidence and antique-store history.

answerKey.method: Swapped the real antique respirator with a doctored display piece that carried trace fibers from the appraisal room
answerKey.motive: To hide that the antique object was tied to an older Circa 1857 ownership dispute
answerKey.keyEvidence: display-case inventory photo, appraisal tag ink, and fibers inside the false respirator
answerKey.explanation: Chapter 4: The Object That Should Not Exist closes one layer of the larger story. This chapter proves the weaponized object came from the antique store and connects the homicide to an older secret. The proof is display-case inventory photo, appraisal tag ink, and fibers inside the false respirator.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Chapter 4: The Object That Should Not Exist: The Circa Ledger. Heresay Speakeasy is attached to Circa 1857 antique store, so this murder is solved by tracking what moves through both spaces. This is one continuous story; each chapter gets harder and reveals a deeper layer.
revealScript: Chapter closed. Chapter 4: The Object That Should Not Exist revealed that Victor Harrow was responsible for this layer. Method: Swapped the real antique respirator with a doctored display piece that carried trace fibers from the appraisal room. Motive: To hide that the antique object was tied to an older Circa 1857 ownership dispute. Key proof: display-case inventory photo, appraisal tag ink, and fibers inside the false respirator. Larger-story meaning: This chapter proves the weaponized object came from the antique store and connects the homicide to an older secret.
